content.heading: The Brief
content.body: A leading East African financial institution needed a headquarters that would signal leadership, ambition, and permanence. The brief called for executive-grade interiors across five floors — from a commanding ground-floor reception through to private boardrooms and the CEO suite — all unified by a single material palette and a clear spatial hierarchy.

content.caption: The double-height reception lobby sets the tone for the entire building.

items.heading: Design Challenge
items.body: The building shell was a standard commercial fit-out with low ceilings and a fragmented floor plate. Creating a sense of grandeur within these constraints required precise material choices and deliberate spatial compression and release.

items.heading: Our Approach
items.body: We introduced a continuous veined marble motif across floor and feature walls, drew the eye upward with vertical brass fins, and used acoustic baffling as a design element. Each floor has a distinct identity while remaining part of the whole.

content.caption: The executive corridor on the fifth floor — restrained and deliberately quiet.

content.heading: The Result
content.body: A headquarters that reads as intentional and assured. The client reported a measurable impact on client confidence during first site visits, and the space has since been featured in two regional architecture publications.
